exemplar.title: Investigating Curly/coily Hair and the Impact that it has on Social Aspects

detailedExemplarData.title: Investigating Curly/coily Hair and the Impact that it has on Social Aspects

strengths: Engaged in interviews with participants and described a research process to inform the product
weaknesses: No explicit identification or reflection on which ATL skills were used in research or product development
Research questions table is incomplete, limiting the breadth of investigation
Sources rely heavily on commercial blogs; lacks peer-reviewed or expert publications to support credibility
Opportunities to connect communication and information-literacy skills to specific research tasks were missed
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The student has undertaken important research activities, but has not linked these efforts to specific ATL skills or provided evidence of their impact. Naming and reflecting on these skillsâsuch as information literacy in source evaluation or communication in conducting interviewsâwould strengthen the application section.

strengths: Effective linking of the global context to the product, demonstrating deep understanding of the cultural significance of curly/coily hair
Compelling personal anecdote that grounds the project in genuine interest and supports sustained engagement
Detailed action plan outlining phases of work
weaknesses: Confusing plan table structure with empty headers and duplicated entries, which obscures sequencing
Overly broad scope by including both a booklet and a hair-care product, making the project less feasible
Learning goal and success criteria lack specific, measurable targets (e.g., number of interviews, design standards)
Timeline omits resources, tools, and personnel required for each phase
Design-cycle strands are mentioned but not clearly mapped to planning steps
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: While the student shows strong personal engagement and has sketched out phases of work, the planning documentation requires greater clarity and focus. A streamlined, well-labelled table with defined deliverables, measurable criteria, and linked resources would make the plan more feasible and assessable.

strengths: Reflection section is clearly labeled and organized, enhancing readability
Insightful discussion of emotional and personal growth shows increasing self-awareness and resilience
weaknesses: Does not evaluate the final product against each success criterion with supporting evidence
Reflection on product quality remains general rather than tied to the defined success metrics
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The student demonstrates thoughtful personal insight and organizes their reflection well. To complete this strand, they need to rigorously evaluate the deliverable against each success criterion, using concrete examples or data to show achievement and areas for refinement.

annotations.comment: Plan table structure is confusing due to empty headers and cells. Simplify the format for clearer sequencing and readability.
Effective linking of the global context to your product demonstrates deep understanding of the cultural significance of curly/coily hair.
You outline both a booklet and a hair care product, which broadens scope. Refine by specifying exact deliverables and limiting the scope to keep the project feasible.
Tasks for product creation are outlined but do not reference ATL skills used. Reflect on which skills you applied and how they supported your design process.
Clear labeling of the Reflection section enhances readability and indicates thoughtful organization of your evaluative process.

Your learning goal clearly states the desired booklet product but lacks measurable success criteria (e.g., number of interviews, length, design standards). Define specific targets to assess achievement.
Your research questions table begins with one question but lacks a full set of inquiries. Develop a comprehensive list to guide a thorough investigation.
Strong personal anecdote illustrates your motivation, grounding the project in genuine interest and supporting sustained engagement.
Your rationale for success criteria explains intent but needs to translate these into specific, measurable targets for each category.
Your rich background narrative is engaging, but focus more on planning elements (resources, milestones) directly tied to product development.
The initial criteria table covers only 0 and 1â2 levels. Expand descriptors to cover the full achievement spectrum and ensure clarity at each level.
A comprehensive criteria table is present but entries appear duplicated and truncated. Streamline the layout and clearly label each performance band.
Your timeline overview distributes tasks by month but omits specific resources, tools, or personnel needed for each phase. Add these details to strengthen feasibility.
Detailed action plan is a strength, yet reflection cells for some phases are blank. Complete all reflections to evaluate progress and adjust your plan.
Reference to design cycle strands is brief. Specify which design steps will be adapted and explain how they guide your product development.
You describe your research process but do not explicitly name which ATL skills (e.g., information literacy) you applied or provide concrete examples of their impact.
Interviewing participants is valuable, but connect this to specific communication ATL skills and describe how their use improved your data quality.
Your reflection identifies personal learning but does not evaluate the final product against each success criterion. Include specific evidence to demonstrate how each criterion was met.
Strong reflection on emotional and personal impact demonstrates growth in self-awareness, resilience, and genuine appreciation for your research journey.
Most sources are from commercial blogs; strengthen your research by incorporating peer-reviewed articles or expert publications to improve credibility.
